steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The skill is Words in Context. The blank appears in "diadromous fish can be ______ those that are merely euryhaline." The passage draws a contrast between obligate migrators and salt-tolerant non-migrators, so the blank must mean clearly separated or distinguished from.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — demarcated from: Demarcated means set apart by a clear boundary or distinction. “Diadromous fish can be demarcated from those that are merely euryhaline” precisely captures the contrast the passage draws between obligate migrators and tolerant non-migrators. ✓
  • Choice B — reconstituted as: Reconstituted means reformed or restructured into something else. The passage is not describing a transformation of diadromous fish into euryhaline fish or any other form. ✗
  • Choice C — derived from: Derived from means originating or descended from. The passage makes no claim that diadromous fish evolved from euryhaline ones; it only contrasts their behaviors. ✗
  • Choice D — conflated with: Conflated means wrongly merged or confused with. The passage is doing the opposite — it is drawing a distinction, not collapsing one. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
